halfLife.conditions: healthy adults; label states an elimination half-life of 61.3 minutes (~1.0 h)

vd.conditions: apparent volume of distribution ~0.3–0.4 L/kg (SmPC); point value 0.35 L/kg reproduces the label's 500 mg oral peak (~5.5–7.5 µg/mL) under this one-compartment fit. Scaled to absolute litres against the 70 kg reference subject at derivation time.

F.conditions: SmPC states amoxicillin is approximately 70% orally bioavailable; other sources report up to ~89% for doses ≤ 2 g. Absorption is not affected by food.

tmax.conditions: peak blood levels 1–2 h after oral administration (label); SmPC median ~1 h. Point value 1 h. Note ke (from a ~1 h half-life) is close to ka here, so this drug sits near the ka≈ke absorption/elimination boundary.

fda_label.title: Amoxicillin Capsule (NorthStar Rx LLC; mfr Aurobindo Pharma) — FDA Structured Product Label, clinical pharmacology (half-life, Tmax, peak levels, protein binding)

smpc.title: Amoxicillin 500 mg Capsules — Summary of Product Characteristics (emc), section 5.2 pharmacokinetic properties (bioavailability, volume of distribution)

notes: Curated from the FDA Structured Product Label for amoxicillin capsules (NorthStar/Aurobindo — half-life 61.3 min, Tmax 1–2 h, 250/500 mg peak blood levels, ~20% protein binding) plus the EMA SmPC (medicines.org.uk) for the apparent volume of distribution (~0.3–0.4 L/kg) and oral bioavailability (~70%), which the US label does not quantify. ke is taken from the ~1 h half-life. Amoxicillin is US oral-only (its IV sibling is ampicillin), so only the oral route is offered. LINEARITY: within 250–3000 mg the bioavailability (AUC) is proportional to dose and elimination is first-order and renal (dose-independent half-life), so identical repeated doses superpose and linear: true. The one documented nonlinearity is in the absorption RATE, not in AUC or elimination: at higher single doses the absorption rate saturates, which flattens Cmax (sub-proportional) and pushes Tmax later. That means the single ka used here is valid for the illustrative dose but would overstate the absorption rate at much larger doses — a shape caveat, not a superposition-breaking nonlinearity. A curatorial cross-check: Vd 0.35 L/kg with F 0.70 reproduces the label's reported 500 mg peak (~7 µg/mL) under this one-compartment model, which is why that Vd point value was chosen within the 0.3–0.4 range. Because the half-life (~1 h) makes ke comparable to the absorption rate ka, amoxicillin is a good illustration of near-flip-flop kinetics. Educational illustration only — not for dosing.
